findings.issue: Section order deviates from template. Expected order places 'Context Files' before 'Verification Steps' (template positions 7 and 8). In task 04 the order is Description -> What to do -> Acceptance Criteria -> Verification Steps -> Details -> Context Files -> Reviewers. 'Context Files' appears after 'Details', and 'Acceptance Criteria' precedes 'Verification Steps' without a Context Files block in between.
findings.fix: Reorder sections to template sequence: Required Skills, Description, What to do, (TDD Anchor if code), Acceptance Criteria, Context Files, Verification Steps, Details, Reviewers, Post-completion. Move the 'Context Files' block up to before 'Verification Steps'.

findings.issue: Inline comment on the depends_on line still reads the template placeholder '# ID задач-зависимостей (строки: ["01", "02"])' while the actual value is []. The value is correct; only the leftover example comment is cosmetic.
findings.fix: Optional: trim the stale example from the comment for clarity. Not blocking.

findings.issue: Feature-artifact and code-file links use repo-root-relative paths (e.g. docs/features/.../001-feat-backend-killer.md, backendkiller/backendkiller.go) while tasks 02/03/04 use paths relative to the task file's directory (e.g. 001-feat-backend-killer.md, ../../../cmd/main.go). Both styles resolve, but the batch is internally inconsistent.
findings.fix: Pick one link-path convention across the batch (relative-to-task-file is what 02/03/04 use). Cosmetic; links are valid as-is.

decomposition.graph_detail: 01: wave1 depends [] ; 02: wave2 depends [01] (wave 2 > 1, ok) ; 03: wave2 depends [] (independent, ok) ; 04: wave3 depends [01,02,03] (wave 3 > all dep waves, ok). No cycles. All depends_on IDs reference existing tasks.
decomposition.atomicity: Task 01 is large (full package + 3 files + tests) but logically cohesive single unit (one workload package). Tasks 02 (CLI wiring, 2 files), 03 (README, 1 file), 04 (QA) each one responsibility. No merge or split candidates.
decomposition.traceability: Files-to-modify in each task match the tech-spec Implementation Tasks entries (Task 1: backendkiller/*.go; Task 2: cmd/main.go + cmd/app.go; Task 3: README.md; Task 4: no code). No tech-spec files dropped or unexplained new files.

skill_reviewer_mapping.task_01: skills=[code-writing] -> reviewers=[dev-code-reviewer, dev-security-auditor, dev-test-reviewer] (correct: code-writing requires all three)
skill_reviewer_mapping.task_04: skills=[pre-deploy-qa] -> reviewers=[] (correct: self-verifying QA task, no reviewers)

carry_forward.acceptance_criteria: Tech-spec AC fully carried into tasks. Task 01 carries all package/behavioral AC; Task 02 carries the 5-flags/no-jobs AC; Task 03 carries README AC; Task 04 aggregates the full user-spec + tech-spec AC for QA verification. No AC dropped.
carry_forward.tdd_anchor: Tech-spec Testing Strategy items (TestConfig_validate table test, heavy-PREPARE builder unit test, TestWorkload_Run testcontainers mechanics + invalid-conninfo) all present in Task 01 TDD Anchor. Non-code tasks 02/03/04 correctly use manual verification instead of TDD Anchor (task 02 keeps an explicit manual-verification anchor in place of the TDD section; tasks 03/04 omit it).
